+++
title = "Local Groups Advocate for Community Energy Initiatives"
date = "2026-03-06"
author = "Sarah Jenkins, Chief Reporter"
categories = ["Community"]
tags = ["Hastings","Energy Policy","Renewable Energy","Sustainability"]
draft = false
+++

In a concerted effort to promote sustainability and local economic growth, several community groups in Hastings have come together to urge the local council to support community energy projects on a larger scale. The initiative seeks to empower local residents by providing them with greater control over their energy sources through renewable methods such as solar panels and wind turbines.

Organisers believe that unlocking this potential will not only benefit the environment but also create opportunities for job creation within the region. They have called upon councillors to facilitate discussions on how best to implement these schemes in a way that is both efficient and accessible to all members of the community.

The move comes as part of an ongoing push towards more sustainable practices across East Sussex, with similar calls being made by other towns and cities within the county.

---
**Source:** [Original Article](https://news.google.?oc=5)
